title: Definitions
content: Key definitions including: 'associate company' - company with significant influence; 'body corporate' - includes company incorporated outside India; 'books of account' - includes electronic records; 'director' - person appointed to Board; 'financial year' - April 1 to March 31; 'key managerial personnel' - CEO, MD, CFO, CS; 'one person company' - company with only one member; 'private company' - restricts transfer of shares, limits members to 200; 'public company' - not a private company; 'related party' - director, KMP, relatives, holding/subsidiary companies.

title: Formation of Company
content: A company may be formed for any lawful purpose by: (a) 7 or more persons for public company; (b) 2 or more persons for private company; (c) 1 person for One Person Company. Company may be: limited by shares, limited by guarantee, or unlimited. Memorandum subscribed by all members.

title: Memorandum of Association
content: Memorandum shall state: (a) name with 'Limited' or 'Private Limited'; (b) State of registered office; (c) objects of company; (d) liability of members - limited/unlimited; (e) authorized share capital and division into shares; (f) subscriber details with shares taken and signatures.

title: Articles of Association
content: Articles contain regulations for management of company. May adopt Table F (Schedule I) for company limited by shares. Must include: entrenchment provisions (if any), number of members in private company, restriction on transfer of shares in private company.

title: Incorporation of Company
content: File with Registrar: memorandum and articles, declaration by professional (CA/CS/Advocate), affidavit from subscribers, address proof of registered office, particulars of first directors with consent, particulars of subscribers. Registrar shall register and issue Certificate of Incorporation with CIN.

title: Formation of Company with Charitable Objects
content: Company may be formed for promoting commerce, art, science, sports, education, research, social welfare, religion, charity, protection of environment. No dividend to members. Profits applied for promoting objects. License from Central Government required. 'Limited' may be dispensed from name.

title: Registered Office of Company
content: Company shall have registered office from 15th day of incorporation. All communications addressed to registered office. Company shall furnish verification of registered office within 30 days of incorporation. Change of registered office: within same State - Board resolution; to another State - special resolution and Central Government approval.

title: Kinds of Share Capital
content: Share capital shall be of two kinds: (a) Equity share capital - with voting rights, or with differential rights as to dividend, voting or otherwise; (b) Preference share capital - preferential right in payment of dividend and repayment of capital. Preference shares to be redeemed within 20 years.

title: Application of Premiums Received on Issue of Shares
content: Securities premium account may be applied for: issuing fully paid bonus shares; writing off preliminary expenses; writing off commission/discount on issue of shares/debentures; providing for premium payable on redemption of preference shares/debentures; buyback of shares.

title: Issue of Sweat Equity Shares
content: Company may issue sweat equity shares to directors or employees for providing know-how, intellectual property rights, or value additions. Requires special resolution. Cannot exceed 25% of paid-up equity capital at any time or 15% in any year. Lock-in period of 3 years.

title: Further Issue of Share Capital
content: First offer to existing shareholders in proportion to paid-up capital (rights issue) - open for minimum 15 days, maximum 30 days. May offer to employees under ESOP. May offer to any persons by special resolution (preferential allotment). Renunciation of rights allowed.

title: Power of Company to Purchase its Own Securities
content: Company may buyback shares if: authorized by articles, special resolution passed (Board resolution if buyback <= 10% of paid-up capital + free reserves), buyback <= 25% of paid-up capital + free reserves, debt-equity ratio post-buyback <= 2:1, shares fully paid-up, completed within 1 year.

title: Prohibition on Acceptance of Deposits from Public
content: Company shall not accept deposits from public except in accordance with Chapter V. Private company may accept deposits from members. Deposit repayment reserve of 20% of deposits maturing during year. Deposit insurance required. Interest rate not exceeding RBI prescribed rate.

title: Annual Return
content: Every company shall prepare annual return (Form MGT-7) containing: registered office, principal business activities, particulars of holding/subsidiary companies, shares/debentures, members/debenture holders, promoters/directors/KMP, meetings held, remuneration of directors/KMP. File within 60 days of AGM.

title: Annual General Meeting
content: Every company (except OPC) shall hold AGM each year. Gap between two AGMs not more than 15 months. First AGM within 9 months from close of first financial year. Subsequent AGMs within 6 months from close of financial year. Held on working day during business hours (9 AM to 6 PM) at registered office or within city/town of registered office.

title: Calling of Extraordinary General Meeting
content: Board may call EGM when deemed fit. Board shall call EGM on requisition of members holding >= 1/10th of paid-up share capital having voting rights. If Board fails to call within 21 days, requisitionists may call meeting within 3 months of requisition.

title: Statement to be Annexed to Notice
content: Statement of material facts concerning each item of special business shall be annexed to notice. Includes: nature of concern or interest of every director/manager/KMP. For director appointment: brief resume, expertise, relationships with other directors, number of board meetings attended, other directorships.

title: Resolutions and Agreements to be Filed
content: Copy of every resolution/agreement required to be filed with Registrar within 30 days: special resolution, resolution requiring special notice, resolution for voluntary winding up, resolution of Board relating to appointment/reappointment/renewal of MD/WTD/manager.

title: Declaration of Dividend
content: Dividend only out of: profits of current year, accumulated profits, money provided by government for payment of dividend. Transfer to reserves before dividend. Deposit in scheduled bank account within 5 days of declaration. Pay within 30 days. No dividend on unpaid calls.

title: Unpaid Dividend Account
content: Dividend unpaid/unclaimed for 30 days shall be transferred to Unpaid Dividend Account within 7 days. If unclaimed for 7 years, transfer to Investor Education and Protection Fund (IEPF). Company to file statement of unclaimed dividend annually.

title: Books of Account
content: Every company shall keep books of account at registered office giving true and fair view of affairs. Accrual basis, double entry system. Books include: cash flow statement, money received/expended, assets and liabilities, cost records (if applicable). Preserve for 8 years.

title: Financial Statement
content: Financial statements to give true and fair view. Includes: Balance Sheet, Profit and Loss Account, Cash Flow Statement (not for OPC, small company, dormant company), Statement of Changes in Equity, explanatory notes. Comply with accounting standards. Consolidated financial statements if holding company.

title: Financial Statement and Board's Report
content: Financial statement signed by chairperson or 2 directors (one being MD). Board's report to include: extract of annual return, number of Board meetings, directors' responsibility statement, particulars of loans/guarantees/investments, related party transactions, state of company affairs, conservation of energy, CSR report (if applicable), corporate governance report (if applicable).

title: Corporate Social Responsibility
content: Applicable if: net worth >= Rs. 500 crore OR turnover >= Rs. 1000 crore OR net profit >= Rs. 5 crore in preceding financial year. Constitute CSR Committee with 3+ directors (1 independent). Spend >= 2% of average net profits of preceding 3 years on CSR activities in Schedule VII. Unspent amount to be transferred to Fund or Unspent CSR Account.

title: Copy of Financial Statement to be Filed with Registrar
content: File with Registrar within 30 days of AGM: financial statements (including consolidated), duly adopted at AGM, along with Board's report and auditor's report. Form AOC-4. Additional fee for delay: 2x normal fee for first 30 days, 4x for next 30 days, etc.

title: Internal Audit
content: Prescribed class of companies shall appoint internal auditor (CA or cost accountant or other professional). Applicable to: listed company, unlisted public company with paid-up capital >= Rs. 50 crore or turnover >= Rs. 200 crore or outstanding loans/deposits >= Rs. 100 crore, private company with turnover >= Rs. 200 crore or outstanding loans >= Rs. 100 crore.

title: Appointment of Auditors
content: Appoint at first AGM to hold office till conclusion of 6th AGM (5 years). Subsequent appointment for 5 years. Listed company: same auditor/audit firm not for more than 2 consecutive terms of 5 years. Rotation: individual auditor - 1 term of 5 years; audit firm - 2 consecutive terms of 5 years. Cooling period: 5 years. First auditor appointed by Board within 30 days of incorporation.

title: Removal, Resignation of Auditor
content: Removal before expiry of term by special resolution with Central Government approval. Auditor may resign by filing ADT-3 with Registrar within 30 days stating reasons. Cannot resign during audit. Casual vacancy: Board (other than resignation) or general meeting (resignation) within 30 days.

title: Eligibility, Qualifications and Disqualifications of Auditors
content: Only CA (individual or firm) can be auditor. Disqualified if: body corporate, officer/employee of company, partner/employee of officer/employee, indebted to company > Rs. 5 lakh, given guarantee/security > Rs. 1 lakh, has business relationship, relative is director/KMP, full time employment elsewhere, convicted of fraud (10 years).

title: Powers and Duties of Auditors
content: Auditor has right of access to all books, vouchers, information. Auditor's report shall state: whether proper books of account maintained, financial statements give true and fair view, Balance Sheet/P&L agree with books, compliance with accounting standards, adequacy of internal financial controls, company's affairs not prejudiced. Report on fraud to Central Government.

title: Company to Have Board of Directors
content: Minimum directors: 3 (public), 2 (private), 1 (OPC). Maximum: 15 (can increase by special resolution). At least 1 director resident in India (stayed 182+ days in previous year). Listed company: at least 1/3rd independent directors. Woman director: prescribed class of companies.

title: Manner of Selection of Independent Directors
content: Independent directors selected from data bank maintained by institute/association. Data bank to contain: name, qualifications, experience. Not applicable if company proposes to appoint person possessing prescribed qualifications. Online proficiency self-assessment test to be passed within 2 years.

title: Appointment of Directors
content: Directors appointed in general meeting. First directors named in articles. 2/3rd of total directors shall be liable to retire by rotation. 1/3rd of rotational directors retire at every AGM. Retiring director eligible for re-appointment. Independent director not liable to retire by rotation. DIN mandatory before appointment.

title: Appointment of Additional Director, Alternate Director and Nominee Director
content: Board may appoint additional director to hold office till next AGM. Alternate director for original director absent for >= 3 months (not for independent director). Nominee director appointed by institution/secured creditor/government. No person to hold alternate directorship for more than one director.

title: Disqualifications for Appointment of Director
content: Disqualified if: unsound mind, undischarged insolvent, convicted of offence involving moral turpitude (5 years not elapsed), court order disqualifying, unpaid calls on shares for 6 months, convicted under this Act (5 years not elapsed), failed to file financial statements/annual returns for 3 consecutive years or repay deposits/interest/dividend.

title: Number of Directorships
content: Maximum 20 companies at same time. Not more than 10 public companies. Alternate directorship counted. Private company directorship excluded from limit of 20. If limit exceeded, choose companies within 1 year and inform other companies.

title: Duties of Directors
content: Director shall: act in accordance with articles, act in good faith for benefit of members/employees/shareholders/community/environment, exercise duties with due care/skill/diligence, not involve in conflict of interest, not achieve undue gain, not assign office. Contravention: fine Rs. 1-5 lakh.

title: Vacation of Office of Director
content: Director vacates office if: disqualified under Section 164, absents from all Board meetings for 12 months, acts against company/State, fails to disclose interest, removed by ordinary resolution (special resolution not required), convicted and sentenced to imprisonment >= 6 months.

title: Resignation of Director
content: Director may resign by giving notice in writing to company. Board to take note and inform Registrar within 30 days (Form DIR-12). Director to file DIR-11 with Registrar. Resignation effective from date stated in notice or date of receipt by company, whichever is later.

title: Meetings of Board
content: First Board meeting within 30 days of incorporation. Minimum 4 meetings per year. Gap between meetings not exceeding 120 days. Notice of minimum 7 days (shorter notice with consent). May be held through video conferencing. Quorum: 1/3rd of total strength or 2 directors, whichever is higher. Interested director not counted in quorum.

title: Quorum for Meetings of Board
content: Quorum: 1/3rd of total strength (not less than 2). Interested director not part of quorum. If quorum not present, meeting adjourned to same day, same time, same place in next week. Adjourned meeting valid even if quorum not present.

title: Audit Committee
content: Mandatory for: listed companies, public companies with paid-up capital >= Rs. 10 crore or turnover >= Rs. 100 crore or loans >= Rs. 50 crore. Minimum 3 directors with majority being independent. Chairperson must be independent. 2/3rd members financially literate. Functions: recommend auditor appointment, review financial statements, approve related party transactions, oversee vigil mechanism.

title: Nomination and Remuneration Committee
content: Mandatory for: listed companies, public companies with paid-up capital >= Rs. 10 crore or turnover >= Rs. 100 crore or loans >= Rs. 50 crore. Minimum 3 non-executive directors with half being independent. Chairperson must be independent. Functions: identify qualified persons for directors, recommend appointment, formulate criteria for evaluation, recommend remuneration policy.

title: Powers of Board
content: Board exercises powers by resolution at meeting. Matters requiring Board resolution: call on shares, authorize buyback, issue securities, borrow money, invest funds, grant loans, approve financial statements, diversify business, approve amalgamation/merger/reconstruction, appoint internal auditor, take note of disclosures by directors.

title: Restrictions on Powers of Board
content: Requires special resolution: sell/lease/dispose whole or substantially whole undertaking, invest (other than trust securities) exceeding limits, borrow exceeding paid-up capital + free reserves, remit/give time for repayment of debt due from director.

title: Loan to Directors
content: Company shall not advance loan to director or person in whom director is interested, or give guarantee/security. Exceptions: (a) loan to MD/WTD as per employment conditions; (b) company in ordinary course of business at prevailing market rate; (c) loan to subsidiary whose director is guarantor.

title: Loan and Investment by Company
content: Limit: 60% of paid-up capital + free reserves OR 100% of free reserves, whichever is more. Beyond limit requires special resolution. Loan to be utilized for principal business. Rate of interest not less than government securities yield. Register of loans/investments to be maintained.

title: Related Party Transactions
content: Company shall not enter into contract/arrangement with related party for: sale/purchase of goods, selling/buying property, leasing, availing/rendering services, agent appointment, office of profit - without Board consent (arm's length/ordinary course of business) or prior approval by special resolution. Disclosure in Board's report.

title: Appointment of Managing Director, Whole-time Director or Manager
content: Cannot appoint both MD and manager simultaneously. Term not exceeding 5 years at a time. Re-appointment not earlier than 1 year before expiry. Special resolution required if: age < 21 or > 70 years.

title: Appointment of Key Managerial Personnel
content: Every listed company and prescribed class of companies shall have whole-time KMP: MD or CEO or manager, company secretary, CFO. Individual shall not be chairperson and MD/CEO at same time unless articles provide otherwise or company has no MD/CEO.

title: Compromise or Arrangement
content: Application to Tribunal for compromise/arrangement by company, creditor, member, or liquidator. Meeting of creditors/members to be called. Approved if 3/4th in value approve. Tribunal may sanction if fair. Notice to Central Government, income-tax authorities, SEBI. Binding on all parties.

title: Merger and Amalgamation
content: Tribunal may order merger of 2+ companies or division of company. Scheme approved by 3/4th majority of each class. Notice to Registrar, income-tax, SEBI, CCI. Transferee company issues shares to transferor company shareholders. Fast track merger between holding-wholly owned subsidiary or small companies permitted.

title: Valuation by Registered Valuers
content: Where valuation required under this Act, it shall be valued by registered valuer who has expertise and qualification. Valuer shall make impartial, true and fair valuation. Shall not undertake valuation of assets in which he has direct/indirect interest. Penalty for contravention.

title: Power of Registrar to Remove Name from Register
content: Registrar may strike off name if: failed to commence business within 1 year of incorporation, not carrying on business for 2 preceding years, subscribers/directors not paid subscription, not carrying on business. 30 days notice to company. Publication in Official Gazette. Liability continues.

title: Circumstances for Winding Up by Tribunal
content: Company may be wound up if: unable to pay debts, special resolution for winding up, acted against sovereignty/integrity of India, Tribunal of opinion it should be wound up, failed to file financial statements/annual returns for 5 consecutive years.

title: Punishment for Fraud
content: Person guilty of fraud: imprisonment 6 months to 10 years AND fine not less than amount involved extending to 3 times the amount. If public interest involved: imprisonment not less than 3 years. Fraud includes: wrongful gain, wrongful loss, inducement for act/omission, injury to interests.

title: Dormant Company
content: Company may obtain dormant status if: formed for future project, holding assets, no significant accounting transaction for 2 years. Maintain minimum number of directors. File annual return. Financial statement. Dormant status for maximum 5 consecutive years. Struck off if inactive.
